# Privacy Gateway

Implement the Oblivious HTTP IETF standard to improve client privacy

> Links below point directly to Markdown versions of each page. Any page can also be retrieved as Markdown by sending an `Accept: text/markdown` header to the page's URL without the `index.md` suffix (for example, `curl -H "Accept: text/markdown" https://developers.cloudflare.com/privacy-gateway/`).
>
> For other Cloudflare products, see the [Cloudflare documentation directory](https://developers.cloudflare.com/llms.txt).

## Overview

- [Cloudflare Privacy Gateway](https://developers.cloudflare.com/privacy-gateway/index.md): Privacy Gateway is a managed Oblivious HTTP (OHTTP) relay service that hides client IP addresses from application backends.

## Get started

- [Get started](https://developers.cloudflare.com/privacy-gateway/get-started/index.md): Set up Privacy Gateway by configuring your server, client, and relay connection using the OHTTP standard.

## reference

- [Legal](https://developers.cloudflare.com/privacy-gateway/reference/legal/index.md): Understand what data Cloudflare sees, stores, and shares when operating the Privacy Gateway relay service.
- [Limitations](https://developers.cloudflare.com/privacy-gateway/reference/limitations/index.md): Known limitations of Privacy Gateway, including the inability to prevent websites from sending identifying user data.
- [Privacy Gateway Metrics](https://developers.cloudflare.com/privacy-gateway/reference/metrics/index.md): Query Privacy Gateway traffic and performance data using the GraphQL Analytics API.
- [Product compatibility](https://developers.cloudflare.com/privacy-gateway/reference/product-compatibility/index.md): Cloudflare products that are and are not compatible with Privacy Gateway, including API Shield, Cache, and WAF.
